pi_items.source: Found in both setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts which explicitly create the EXPORT_DIR at '/home/ga/DICOM/exports' and write the final file there.

Python code simulation of the exact DICOM windowing defined in setup_task.sh (WC=40, WW=400, Intercept=-1024) proves the noisy background mathematically maps to 0 in 8-bit JPEG. The actual edge variance is exactly 0.0 even without a shutter.

Python code simulation of the DICOM standard windowing applied to the synthetic center anatomy yields an 8-bit JPEG variance of approximately 106.1. A threshold of 500 is mathematically impossible to achieve in 8-bit space for this data.

privileged_info_summary: The data is entirely synthetic. The expected output path is verified to be /home/ga/DICOM/exports/collimated_view.jpg. However, the task's expected image variance thresholds are fundamentally flawed due to a misunderstanding of DICOM to 8-bit JPEG windowing. The synthetic background (raw 150) is perfectly clipped to black (0) by the chosen window (WC=40, WW=400, Intercept=-1024), resulting in a pre-shutter edge variance of exactly 0.0, trivializing the <50 threshold. Furthermore, the synthetic central anatomy (raw 1250 +/- 20) yields an 8-bit JPEG variance of ~106.1, making the >500 center variance threshold mathematically impossible to pass.
